geopolitics_summary: Iran war disrupts Suez/Hormuz transit; EU weakens methane rules under US pressure; Italy pivots to Libya gas as alternative supply.

themes.headline: Italy seeks Libyan gas to offset Iran war supply shortage.
themes.why_it_matters: Direct EU gas supply diversification. Libya–Italy pipeline ramp-up could ease TTF if successful, but war-torn country limits production. Key supply backstop under geopolitical stress.

themes.headline: EU Commission weakens methane enforcement under US pressure.
themes.why_it_matters: Relaxed methane rules reduce cost friction on gas supply, indirectly supporting higher gas flows and damping power-curve bullish pressure via fuel-switching risk.

themes.headline: EU floats exempting oil firms from methane rules on energy security grounds.
themes.why_it_matters: Policy flexibility to boost supply during crisis. Lowers compliance cost on imports; supports higher gas volumes entering EU, pressuring TTF and power.

themes.headline: US military secures Strait of Hormuz passage; Lufthansa faces €1.7B jet fuel cost.
themes.why_it_matters: Hormuz reopening reduces crude supply shock; lower crude prices weaken incentive for coal-to-gas switch in EU power. Jet fuel crisis indicates broader energy supply stress affecting European fuel costs.

themes.headline: Fertilizer and fuel price surge threatens European food inflation next year.
themes.why_it_matters: Energy input cost shock into agricultural sector. Indirect: rising fertilizer-production costs signal sustained European natural gas price pressure and demand for power in processing.

watchlist: Hormuz corridor reopening pace and impact on crude–TTF arb next 2 weeks.
EU methane rule exemption guidance release date and scope definition.
Libya gas production ramp timeline for Italy pipeline (production feasibility).
Fertilizer sector gas demand response; industrial load winter 2026–27 forecast.
